content: Viewing the human-language learning practice of deliberate reflection (from the vocabulary learning context of Reflexion) as a blueprint for improving the iterative self-improvement structure of LLM self-refinement can create more human-aligned, generalizable self-improvement loops for large language models. This approach frames structured vocabulary-focused reflection exercises as a template for designing incremental self-refinement checkpoints that teach LLMs to explicitly correct their own linguistic and reasoning gaps the same way human students do.
